annotations.label: Möbius transformation

anchor.snippet: a Möbius transformation of the complex plane is a rational function of the form

annotations.note: The action of `GL (Fin 2) K` on `OnePoint K` (extended Riemann sphere) is defined as Möbius/fractional-linear transformations, generalizing the complex case.

annotations.label: Geometric construction via stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: a Möbius transformation can be obtained by first applying the inverse stereographic projection

annotations.note: Mathlib has `stereographic` in `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Instances.Sphere`, but no result identifying Möbius transformations with sphere rotations composed with stereographic projection.

annotations.label: Angle and generalized-circle preservation

anchor.snippet: These transformations preserve angles, map every straight line to a line or circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has Euclidean inversion (a building block for Möbius maps) but no theorem stating that Möbius transformations preserve angles and generalized circles.

annotations.label: Möbius group as PGL(2,C)

anchor.snippet: They form a group called the Möbius group

annotations.note: `PGL(n, R)` is defined as `GL n R ⧸ center`, but the Möbius group is not introduced as a named object identified with `PGL(2, ℂ)`.

annotations.label: Domain on extended complex plane

anchor.snippet: Möbius transformations are defined on the extended complex plane

annotations.note: `OnePoint K` plays the role of the extended complex plane, and `instGLAction` puts the Möbius action on it.

annotations.label: Möbius transformations are automorphisms of the Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: The Möbius transformations are exactly the bijective conformal maps from the Riemann sphere to itself

annotations.note: No characterization of biholomorphic/conformal automorphisms of the Riemann sphere as Möbius transformations is present.

annotations.label: Isomorphism with isometries of hyperbolic 3-space

anchor.snippet: The Möbius group is isomorphic to the group of orientation-preserving isometries of hyperbolic 3-space

annotations.note: No hyperbolic 3-space is defined in Mathlib, so this isomorphism cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem (higher dimensions)

anchor.snippet: According to Liouville's theorem a Möbius transformation can be expressed as a composition of translations, similarities

annotations.note: Liouville's theorem in conformal geometry is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General form of a Möbius transformation

anchor.snippet: The general form of a Möbius transformation is given by

annotations.note: `smul_some_eq_ite` gives the formula `g • k = (a*k+b)/(c*k+d)` for the action of `GL (Fin 2) K` on `OnePoint K`.

annotations.label: Extension when c ≠ 0

anchor.snippet: this definition is extended to the whole Riemann sphere by defining

annotations.note: The case where the denominator vanishes is handled by the `if g 1 0 * k + g 1 1 = 0 then ∞` branch in `smul_some_eq_ite`.

annotations.label: Extension when c = 0

anchor.snippet: If c = 0 , we define

annotations.note: `smul_infty_eq_ite` says `g • ∞ = ∞` precisely when `g 1 0 = 0`, matching the c = 0 extension.

annotations.label: Bijective holomorphic map of the Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: Thus a Möbius transformation is always a bijective holomorphic function from the Riemann sphere to the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: The action is well-defined and bijective (as a `MulAction`), but holomorphicity on `OnePoint ℂ` is not proven.

annotations.label: Möbius group as a complex Lie group

anchor.snippet: This group can be given the structure of a complex manifold in such a way that composition and inversion are holomorphic maps

annotations.note: No complex Lie group structure on `PGL(2, ℂ)` / Möbius group is constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Degenerate case ad = bc

anchor.snippet: the rational function defined above is a constant

annotations.note: Mathlib's framework requires invertibility (`ad - bc ≠ 0`) via `GL (Fin 2) K`; the degenerate-constant case is excluded by construction rather than proven.

annotations.label: Kernel of the Schwarzian derivative

anchor.snippet: An alternative definition is given as the kernel of the Schwarzian derivative

annotations.note: The Schwarzian derivative is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of two fixed points

anchor.snippet: Every non-identity Möbius transformation has two fixed points

annotations.note: Fixed points are characterized as roots of `fixpointPolynomial`, but the precise statement "two fixed points counted with multiplicity" is not given as a single theorem.

annotations.label: Fixed-point formula via quadratic

anchor.snippet: The fixed points of the transformation

annotations.note: `fixpointPolynomial g = c X^2 + (d-a) X - b` together with `fixpointPolynomial_aeval_eq_zero_iff` encodes the standard fixed-point quadratic.

annotations.label: Linear case when c = 0

anchor.snippet: When c = 0 , the quadratic equation degenerates into a linear equation and the transform is linear

annotations.note: Upper-triangular (c = 0) Möbius transformations are studied in `isParabolic_iff_of_upperTriangular`, but the explicit linearization "transform becomes affine" is not isolated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Simple transformation: translations, rotations, dilations

anchor.snippet: In this case the transformation will be a simple transformation composed of translations

annotations.note: For SL(2,ℝ) with c = 0, the action on ℍ is decomposed as scaling + translation; the general statement is not isolated.

annotations.label: Pure translation when c = 0 and a = d

anchor.snippet: then both fixed points are at infinity, and the Möbius transformation corresponds to a pure translation

annotations.note: `upperRightHom x = [[1,x],[0,1]]` bundles the translation Möbius subgroup as an `AddChar`, capturing this special case.

annotations.label: Topological proof via Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: Topologically, the fact that (non-identity) Möbius transformations fix 2 points

annotations.note: No formalization in Mathlib of this topological argument.

annotations.label: PGL(2,K) is sharply 3-transitive

anchor.snippet: the projective linear group PGL(2, K ) is sharply 3-transitive

annotations.note: Mathlib has `MultipleTransitivity` infrastructure but no theorem that `PGL(2,K)` is sharply 3-transitive on the projective line.

annotations.label: Lefschetz–Hopf fixed-point theorem

anchor.snippet: The Lefschetz–Hopf theorem states that the sum of the indices

annotations.note: The Lefschetz–Hopf fixed-point theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contrast with PGL(2,R)

anchor.snippet: the projective linear group of the real projective line

annotations.note: No comparative result about PGL(2,ℝ) fixed-point behaviour is stated.

annotations.label: Non-parabolic conjugate to dilation/rotation

anchor.snippet: Every non-parabolic transformation is conjugate to a dilation/rotation

annotations.note: No conjugacy normal-form theorem for non-parabolic Möbius transformations is present.

annotations.label: Characteristic constant (multiplier)

anchor.snippet: we can distinguish one of the multipliers

annotations.note: The characteristic constant/multiplier of a Möbius transformation is not defined.

annotations.label: Repulsive and attractive fixed points (loxodromic)

anchor.snippet: For loxodromic transformations, whenever

annotations.note: Attractive/repulsive classification of fixed points is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parabolic normal form as translation

anchor.snippet: In the parabolic case there is only one fixed point

annotations.note: Parabolic matrices are characterized as scalar + nilpotent (`isParabolic_iff_exists`), giving the algebraic content, but conjugacy to a literal translation is not stated.

annotations.label: Translation length

anchor.snippet: Here, β is called the translation length

annotations.note: Translation length is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pole of a Möbius transformation

anchor.snippet: is called the pole of

annotations.note: No notion of `pole of a Möbius transformation` is defined.

annotations.label: Inverse pole

anchor.snippet: is that point to which the point at infinity is transformed

annotations.note: Inverse pole is not defined.

annotations.label: Characteristic parallelogram

anchor.snippet: These four points are the vertices of a parallelogram which is sometimes called the characteristic parallelogram

annotations.note: Characteristic parallelogram is not defined.

annotations.label: Transform specified by two fixed points and pole

anchor.snippet: can be specified with two fixed points

annotations.note: This parametrization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Decomposition as sequence of simple transformations

anchor.snippet: A Möbius transformation can be composed as a sequence of simple transformations

annotations.note: For SL(2,ℝ) with c ≠ 0, Mathlib decomposes the action as scaling-translate-S-translate; the general complex case is not stated.

annotations.label: Simple transformations: translation, rotation, homothety, inversion

anchor.snippet: The following simple transformations are also Möbius transformations

annotations.note: Some special elements (translations via `upperRightHom`, `ModularGroup.S` for inversion, `ModularGroup.T`) appear, but no systematic catalogue.

annotations.label: Explicit composition decomposition

anchor.snippet: Then these functions can be composed

annotations.note: The explicit four-factor decomposition formula is not given.

annotations.label: Equivalence to sequence of simpler transformations

anchor.snippet: A Möbius transformation is equivalent to a sequence of simpler transformations

annotations.note: Decomposition exists only in the SL(2,ℝ)-on-ℍ setting, not in general.

annotations.label: Formula for inverse via composition

anchor.snippet: The existence of the inverse Möbius transformation and its explicit formula are easily derived

annotations.note: Existence of an inverse follows from the `MulAction` of the group `GL (Fin 2) K`; the explicit `(dz - b)/(-cz + a)` formula is not isolated.

annotations.label: Preservation of angles

anchor.section: Preservation of angles and generalized circles
anchor.snippet: the preservation of angles is reduced to proving that circle inversion preserves angles

annotations.note: Euclidean inversion is formalized but angle preservation for Möbius transformations is not stated.

annotations.label: Generalized circles mapped to generalized circles

anchor.section: Preservation of angles and generalized circles
anchor.snippet: Möbius transformations map generalized circles to generalized circles

annotations.note: The image of a sphere under Euclidean inversion is described, but no Möbius-level theorem that generalized circles map to generalized circles is stated.

annotations.label: Cross-ratios are invariant

anchor.snippet: Cross-ratios are invariant under Möbius transformations

annotations.note: Cross-ratio is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross-ratio at infinity by limit

anchor.snippet: then the cross-ratio has to be defined by taking the appropriate limit

annotations.note: No cross-ratio definition or limit extension exists.

annotations.label: Cross-ratio real iff concyclic/collinear

anchor.snippet: The cross ratio of four different points is real if and only if there is a line or a circle passing through them

annotations.note: Not formalized; needs cross-ratio first.

annotations.label: Conjugate points w.r.t. a generalized circle

anchor.snippet: are conjugate with respect to a generalized circle

annotations.note: Conjugate-points-with-respect-to-a-generalized-circle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conjugate w.r.t. a line (symmetric)

anchor.snippet: are conjugate with respect to a line, if they are symmetric with respect to the line

annotations.note: Not formalized in this terminology.

annotations.label: Conjugate w.r.t. a circle (inversion)

anchor.snippet: Two points are conjugate with respect to a circle if they are exchanged by the inversion

annotations.note: Inversion is defined, so points exchanged by it can be expressed, but the `conjugate` concept itself is not isolated.

annotations.label: Möbius transformations preserve conjugation

anchor.snippet: Since Möbius transformations preserve generalized circles and cross-ratios, they also preserve the conjugation

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Natural action of PGL(2,C) equals Möbius action

anchor.section: Isomorphism between the Möbius group and PGL(2, C)
anchor.snippet: The natural action of PGL(2, C ) on the complex projective line

annotations.note: `GL(2,K)` acts via Möbius formulas on `OnePoint K` (and scalars act trivially, so this descends to `PGL`); the equivalence with the action on `ℙ K (Fin 2 → K)` is given via `equivProjectivization_smul`.

annotations.label: Identification of CP^1 with the Riemann sphere

anchor.section: Correspondence between the complex projective line and the Riemann sphere
anchor.snippet: the projective line CP 1 and the Riemann sphere are identified as follows

annotations.note: `equivProjectivization : OnePoint K ≃ ℙ K (Fin 2 → K)` realizes the identification for any division ring, hence for ℂ.

annotations.label: Action of PGL(2,C) on the projective line

anchor.section: Action of PGL(2, C) on the complex projective line
anchor.snippet: Every invertible complex 2×2 matrix

annotations.note: The action of any group acting K-linearly (including GL n K) on `ℙ K V` is given by `Projectivization.instMulAction`; passing to PGL uses the `SL_mulAction_ker` result.

annotations.label: Group isomorphism Möbius ≅ PGL(2,C)

anchor.section: Equivalence with a Möbius transformation on the Riemann sphere
anchor.snippet: this induces an identification of the action of the group of Möbius transformations with the action of PGL(2, C )

annotations.note: No formal isomorphism `MöbiusGroup ≃* PGL(2, ℂ)` is constructed because the Möbius group is not given a separate type.

annotations.label: PGL(2,K) as fractional linear transformations

anchor.snippet: one can similarly identify the group PGL(2, K ) of the projective linear automorphisms with the group of fractional linear transformations

annotations.note: `equivProjectivization_smul` says the `GL(2,K)` action on `OnePoint K` (fractional linear formula) matches the projective-line action; the PGL upgrade is implicit.

annotations.label: SL(2,C) → PGL(2,C) surjection with kernel ±I

anchor.snippet: If one divides

annotations.note: `PSL(n, R) = SL(n,R) ⧸ center` is defined, and over a field with characteristic ≠ 2 the center is `{±I}`, but the map `PSL(2,ℂ) → PGL(2,ℂ)` as an isomorphism is not constructed.

annotations.label: Möbius group is a semisimple non-compact Lie group with π₁ = Z₂

anchor.snippet: This allows showing that the Möbius group is a 3-dimensional complex Lie group

annotations.note: No Lie group structure or fundamental group computation for the Möbius group is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sharp 3-transitivity: unique Möbius transformation from three points

anchor.section: Specifying a transformation by three points
anchor.snippet: there exists precisely one Möbius transformation

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib expressing existence and uniqueness of a Möbius transformation sending any 3 points to any 3 points.

annotations.label: Mapping three points to 0, 1, ∞

anchor.snippet: It is easy to check that the Möbius transformation

annotations.note: The canonical (z₁,z₂,z₃) ↦ (0,1,∞) Möbius map is not formalized.

annotations.label: Anharmonic group

anchor.snippet: is a subgroup known as the anharmonic group

annotations.note: Anharmonic group is not defined.

annotations.label: Explicit determinant formula via hyperbola

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to the equation of a standard hyperbola

annotations.note: This re-expression of `ad - bc = 1` as a hyperbola is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PSL(2,R) as upper half-plane stabilizer

anchor.snippet: we obtain a subgroup of the Möbius group denoted as PSL(2, R )

annotations.note: SL(2,ℝ) (and PSL via the faithful action) acts on `ℍ` via `UpperHalfPlane.SLAction`, but the explicit characterization as the upper-half-plane-preserving subgroup of the Möbius group is not stated.

annotations.label: Open-disk-preserving subgroup

anchor.snippet: The subgroup of all Möbius transformations that map the open disk

annotations.note: Disk-preserving Möbius subgroup is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isomorphism between half-plane and disk subgroups

anchor.snippet: Since both of the above subgroups serve as isometry groups

annotations.note: Cayley-transform isomorphism between disk- and half-plane-preserving subgroups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maximal compact subgroup is PSU(2) ≅ SO(3)

anchor.snippet: A maximal compact subgroup of the Möbius group

annotations.note: No maximal-compact-subgroup or PSU(2)≅SO(3) identification for the Möbius group.

annotations.label: Icosahedral groups and the quintic

anchor.snippet: Icosahedral groups of Möbius transformations were used by Felix Klein

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Modular group PSL(2,Z) and Fuchsian groups

anchor.snippet: we obtain the modular group

annotations.note: The modular group action of SL(2,ℤ) on the upper half plane is formalized via `ModularGroup.SLOnGLPos`, but PSL(2,ℤ) and Fuchsian groups in general are not packaged.

annotations.label: Classification into four types

anchor.snippet: Non-identity Möbius transformations are commonly classified into four types

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsParabolic`, `IsHyperbolic`, `IsElliptic` for 2×2 matrices, but does not include `loxodromic` as a separate class.

annotations.label: Trace invariant under conjugation; conjugacy criterion

anchor.snippet: The four types can be distinguished by looking at the trace

annotations.note: Conjugation invariance of these classes is proved (`isParabolic_conj_iff`, `isElliptic_conj_iff`, `isHyperbolic_conj_iff`); the trace-based criterion is implicit via the discriminant but not packaged.

annotations.label: Parabolic transformation

anchor.snippet: is said to be parabolic if

annotations.note: `IsParabolic m := m ∉ Set.range (scalar _) ∧ m.discr = 0` matches the standard definition.

annotations.label: Parabolic iff exactly one fixed point

anchor.snippet: A Möbius transform is parabolic if and only if it has exactly one fixed point

annotations.note: `IsParabolic.smul_eq_self_iff` says the unique fixed point of a parabolic `g` on `OnePoint K` is `parabolicFixedPoint g`.

annotations.label: Parabolic subgroup as unipotent radical

anchor.snippet: The set of all parabolic Möbius transformations with a given fixed point

annotations.note: `upperRightHom` provides a one-parameter subgroup of unipotent (translation-like) matrices, but no general theorem identifying the parabolic-with-given-fixed-point subgroup as unipotent.

annotations.label: Characteristic constant for non-parabolic

anchor.snippet: All non-parabolic transformations have two fixed points and are defined by a matrix conjugate to

annotations.note: Characteristic constant / multiplier λ for non-parabolic transformations is not defined.

annotations.label: Elliptic transformation

anchor.snippet: The transformation is said to be elliptic

annotations.note: `IsElliptic m := m.discr < 0` over an ordered ring matches the standard elliptic condition.

annotations.label: Elliptic iff |λ| = 1 and λ ≠ ±1

anchor.snippet: A transform is elliptic if and only if

annotations.note: `IsElliptic` is defined by discriminant sign; the eigenvalue-based characterization (|λ|=1, λ≠±1) is not stated.

annotations.label: Circular transform and three transpositions fixing {0,1,∞}

anchor.snippet: is also denoted as a circular transform

annotations.note: Circular transforms / anharmonic-group examples are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic transformation

anchor.snippet: The transform is said to be hyperbolic

annotations.note: `IsHyperbolic m := 0 < m.discr` matches the standard hyperbolic condition.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic iff λ is real and λ ≠ ±1

anchor.snippet: A transform is hyperbolic if and only if

annotations.note: Discriminant-based definition is present, but eigenvalue-based criterion is not.

annotations.label: Loxodromic transformation

anchor.snippet: The transform is said to be loxodromic

annotations.note: There is no `IsLoxodromic` class in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification over the reals

anchor.section: The real case and a note on terminology
anchor.snippet: Over the real numbers (if the coefficients must be real), there are no non-hyperbolic loxodromic transformations

annotations.note: Over an ordered ring only `IsParabolic`/`IsHyperbolic`/`IsElliptic` are defined; the loxodromic-only-over-ℂ remark is implicit.

annotations.label: Logarithmic form of the characteristic constant

anchor.section: Geometric interpretation of the characteristic constant
anchor.snippet: The characteristic constant can be expressed in terms of its logarithm

annotations.note: Not formalized; depends on characteristic constant.

annotations.label: Elliptic: ρ = 0, indifferent fixed points

anchor.snippet: then the fixed points are neither attractive nor repulsive but indifferent

annotations.note: `IsElliptic.smul_ne_self` shows elliptic elements have no fixed point on `OnePoint K` for ordered K (i.e., over ℝ); attractive/repulsive analysis over ℂ is not present.

annotations.label: Elliptic one-parameter subgroup along nested circles

anchor.snippet: If we take the one-parameter subgroup generated by any elliptic Möbius transformation

annotations.note: One-parameter subgroup dynamics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Physical interpretation: rotating observer

anchor.snippet: Imagine that some observer rotates with constant angular velocity

annotations.note: Informal physical illustration, not a theorem.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic: α = 0, motion between fixed points

anchor.snippet: then the transformation is said to be hyperbolic

annotations.note: Dynamic description of hyperbolic transformations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic one-parameter subgroup along circular arcs

anchor.snippet: If we take the one-parameter subgroup generated by any hyperbolic Möbius transformation

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Physical interpretation: accelerating observer

anchor.snippet: Imagine that an observer accelerates

annotations.note: Informal physical illustration.

annotations.label: Loxodromic: both nonzero, S-shaped paths

anchor.snippet: If both ρ and α are nonzero, then the transformation is said to be loxodromic

annotations.note: Loxodromic class and its dynamics are absent.

annotations.label: Loxodromic one-parameter subgroup as spiral

anchor.snippet: If we take the one-parameter subgroup generated by any loxodromic Möbius transformation

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Physical interpretation: rotating and translating observer

anchor.snippet: You can probably guess the physical interpretation

annotations.note: Informal physical illustration.

annotations.label: Iteration formula via characteristic constant

anchor.snippet: If a transformation

annotations.note: Iteration formula expressed via the multiplier is not formalized.

annotations.label: Möbius transformation in higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: In higher dimensions, a Möbius transformation is a homeomorphism

annotations.note: Higher-dimensional Möbius transformations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem in conformal geometry

anchor.snippet: Liouville's theorem in conformal geometry states that in dimension at least three

annotations.note: Liouville's theorem in conformal geometry is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orientation-preserving form connected component

anchor.snippet: The orientation-preserving Möbius transformations form the connected component of the identity

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Möbius geometry on the n-sphere

anchor.snippet: The n -sphere, together with action of the Möbius group, is a geometric structure

annotations.note: Möbius geometry as a Klein-geometry structure is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isomorphism with restricted Lorentz group

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism of the Möbius group with the Lorentz group was noted by several authors

annotations.note: Lorentz group is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski space with quadratic form

anchor.snippet: Minkowski space consists of the four-dimensional real coordinate space

annotations.note: Minkowski space with the (+−−−) quadratic form is not defined.

annotations.label: Timelike, spacelike, null cone, celestial sphere

anchor.snippet: Borrowing terminology from special relativity

annotations.note: Special-relativistic vocabulary is not formalized.

annotations.label: SO+(1,3) ≅ PSL(2,C) via hermitian matrices

anchor.snippet: the group of transformations SO + (1, 3) is identified with the group PSL(2, C )

annotations.note: This identification is not formalized.

annotations.label: Möb(n) and isomorphism with SO+(1,n+1)

anchor.snippet: the Möbius group Möb( n ) is the group of all orientation-preserving conformal isometries

annotations.note: Higher-dimensional Möbius group `Möb(n)` is not defined.

annotations.label: Möbius group equals isometries of hyperbolic 3-space

anchor.snippet: the Möbius group is equal to the group of orientation-preserving isometries of hyperbolic 3-space

annotations.note: Hyperbolic 3-space is not defined, so this identification is not formalized.
